evals.prompt: /collab "Crazy thought — what if we let buyers submit their own ideas via QR code at events?"
expectations: Skill classifies as 'idea' (high confidence, ≥0.80)
Generates collab_id with format CL-{THEME}-{NNNN}
Files via GitHub Issue with label:idea
Inserts row into collab_log with classified_type='idea'
Dispatches Telegram message to other founder with 🟢 idea icon

evals.prompt: /collab "Riley just signed off on the JV layer — he's pushing all his deals through us"
expectations: Skill classifies as 'news' (high confidence)
Detects 'Riley' as partner name (95%+ similarity match against partners)
Theme detected as 'PARTNER' → ID format CL-PARTNER-NNNN
Appends to strategy/decisions-log.md (news routing)
Inserts collab_relationships row with relation_kind='partner', relation_target=<partner_uuid>
Telegram dispatch shows '📎 Riley' inline

evals.prompt: /collab "Sam, can you call Mike about the deal terms before Friday"
expectations: Skill detects mode='handoff' (phrasing 'Sam, can you' triggers handoff inference)
Bypasses outbound classification — files as a handoff task
Inserts collab_log with mode='handoff', recipient_github='teammate-handle'
Adds to recipient's task surface (admin portal queue OR daily-plan task list)
Telegram dispatch uses ⚡ handoff icon
Classification confidence is below 0.80 (input is too ambiguous)
Skill DOES NOT silently file — instead asks user to clarify
Output presents top 2 guesses with confidence percentages
Output offers all 8 type options
Output mentions override syntax: /collab as <type> "..."

evals.prompt: /collab as OVS "We should not be building a future feature before /collab itself ships"
expectations: Skill respects explicit type override 'OVS'
was_overridden=true in collab_log row
Skips classification confidence check entirely
Routes per OVS type (decisions-log.md append + GitHub Issue label:strategic)
Skill enters PULL-IN mode (not SUBMIT)
Loads collab record from collab_log
Reads RECIPIENT's local context (current ROADMAP.md, CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, recent commits, open PRs)
Cross-references against local context — surfaces matching ROADMAP items, partner records, open PRs
Proposes 4-6 ranked integration options including 'Custom' fallback
Does NOT execute integration without recipient confirmation
On chosen option: updates collab_log status to 'integrated', notifies sender via Telegram
Lists all open collab_log rows where recipient_github = current founder
Groups by age: 🚨 >72h, ⚠️ 24-72h, 🆕 <24h
Shows collab_id, type, brief summary, sender name, age
Suggests /collab pull <id> as next action

evals.prompt: What's the average ARV for properties in 85003 ZIP?
I'm getting a 500 error from process-submission edge function, can you investigate?

expectations: If gh CLI not authenticated: HALT with clear error 'Cannot resolve current user via gh api. Authenticate gh first.'
If team.json missing: HALT with error '/collab requires founder identity registry'
If founder not in team.json: HALT explaining how to add entry
Does NOT silently fail or skip identity check — identity is foundational

evals.prompt: /collab "the buyer dashboard is showing 0 deals on my Mac since this morning"
expectations: Classifies as 'issue' (high confidence)
Theme detected as 'OPS' (keyword 'showing 0 deals' / 'broken' pattern)
Routes via GitHub Issue with label:bug
Auto-tags affected files via path heuristics (src/components/buyer/, src/hooks/usePropertyMatches.ts)

evals.prompt: /collab "Q3 rock — get 5 institutional buyers signed and ingesting deals daily"
expectations: Classifies as 'rock' (high confidence)
Theme detected as 'ROCK'
Routes via bb_roadmap_activity insert (rock routing per spec §3)
Files GitHub Issue with label:rock as secondary destination
Telegram dispatch uses 🟡 rock icon

validation_notes: Eval pass criteria target: ≥80% expectations met across all should-trigger evals
Negative cases (should_trigger=false) verify the description doesn't over-trigger on adjacent intents (debugging, data queries, etc.)
Identity resolution test (eval-010) is critical — silent failure is the worst outcome since collab without attribution is meaningless
Pull-in mode test (eval-006) verifies the recipient-context-aware integration is honored — this is the key v2 design piece
